titleStmt.title: Story Harran
SHAWI Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the FWF Austrian Science Fund under the number P 33574

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the SHAWI teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the SHAWI network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the dict prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a entry in the SHAWI glossary.
Private URIs using the rend prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a rendition element in VICAV rendition library.
Private URIs using the assets prefix are pointers to the location of the SHAWI media assets.

spanGrp.span: One day, the Falālīḥ family came to the village.
Yes.
They had a very beautiful daughter, the girl was very beautiful. God created her beautifully.
So, all the young men tried to get close to her. Do you listen to me?
Yes. God …
But the girl didn’t want them.
One day, in former times the veterinary officers used to come; and they would eat and take the thingy.
The sheep.
The sheep.
They would take a paper.
Like veterinaries.
Yes.
Like veterinaries.
Look, my brother. A man named ᵊMḥimmad Gḏād from Bani ʕIǧil tribe came.
Yes.
He was a relative of this man here; and Ḥayātīn from the ʕIǧil tribe of Harran, right?
From the clan of Rāfiʕ Efendi.
Yes, yes.
The veterinary officer. The veterinary.
Yes, yes.
He said, “Whose sheep are these?” They told him, “They belong to the ʕAbbādi clan.” He said, “The owner must bring the papers and go to the mountain.”
When I told this to my uncle he said to me, “Mount the horse and go!”
When we want to take the horse’s bridle it turned out he had taken the tether only.
Oh!
When I went to remove the horse’s iron, it turned out that there was no bridle but that I had only the tether in my hands.
Oh!
The snaffle.
The snaffle, dude! It was springtime a and we had a thoroughbred mare. So, I mounted it and rode off.
In the mountain, there was a shrine called ᵊŠkafte. There they were and this was Ḥayātīn.
When your relatives saw the fast-running mare and me on it: young and with my hair down.
Yes.
You hear me, right?
Yes.
And he was with ʕĪsa Efendi, the uncle of the village headman.
Yes
They are from the ʕAlīlaw clan. You know them, right?
Yes, by the Veiler (~ God)! They are my relatives.
He said, “Who is this? ” This is ʕUbēd al-ᵊMḥimmad, ᵊḤsēn aṣ-Ṣāliḥ.
Ooh!
He is the chief of a tribe.
Yes, he is a chieftain.
One of the former chieftains.
Yes.
ʕĪsa Efendi then said, “Hey, this is the son of ʕAbbādi.”
Yes.
He said, “And I supposed that ʕUbēd al-ᵊMḥimmad, the brother of Šaqha, came to us.”
Get off the horse, guy!
ᵊḤsēn aṣ-Ṣāliḥ, the father of ʕAmše!
Ḥsēn aṣ-Ṣāliḥ, the father of ʕAmše. Anyway, I got off the horse.

I said to him, “My brother!” and gave him the veterinary officer‘s paper. When I was on my way back, there were three young girls gathering something on the path. I don’t know if it was wood or thorns. I don’t remember.
Two of them went away.
And one stayed on the path.
One stayed on the path. The path was narrow and after it there was the valley.
Then I could hear this girl, ha!
Nobody ever dared to approach her.
Nobody got close to her, nobody dared to (talk to) her.
By God, I came close and passed by when she said, “I see you didn’t greet me.” I said “Actually I was afraid to greet you and you wouldn’t return my greeting; and that would only make things worse.”
Yes.
She said “No, no! I will return your greeting.”
Only because you are rich.
Anyway, I stopped immediately and said, “Tell me! What do you want?”
She said, “The so-and-so wants me. Perhaps you have already heard about it. But I will not touch him.” Nor the other, I don’t know who else (she mentioned).
“They are all ugly”, she said, “You know, you’re not like them, you’re not ugly. And because you are rich,”
I want you!
“They are all poor.”
Dirt-poor.
“Dirt-poor. But because you are rich, I want you.”
And time passed.
Because he had sheep and camels.
Yes, Sheep and camels.
Yes, yes.
Of course, back then, livestock was precious.
Yes.
